page: Microsoft Azure B32als_v2 is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the Basv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with a base clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The server is configured with 32 virtual CPUs mapped to 16 physical cores, supporting hyper-threading with two threads per core. Memory capacity is 64.0 GB, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

In benchmark testing, the B32als_v2 demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng and strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Passmark memory and encryption tests also rank in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance, static web serving throughput, and Redis SET operations fall within the average range. Large-scale LLM inference, such as prompt processing with a 70B model, is weak, though text generation with a small 135M model is strong.

The primary tradeoff of this server is its burstable CPU allocation and low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. This makes it a cost-efficient option for workloads with variable CPU utilization that do not require sustained full-core performance or massive memory footprints. It is suited for workloads such as software compilation, text processing, lightweight database operations, and web serving where resource demands spike periodically.
description: Microsoft Azure B32als_v2 is a burstable virtual machine from the Basv2 family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with an AMD EPYC 7763 processor featuring 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores, running at 3.525 GHz on an x86_64 architecture. The instance includes 64.0 GB of system memory, representing a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, and lacks local storage or integrated GPUs, though it supports Premium Storage. Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and strong Passmark memory scores, while multi-core and database operations remain average. Due to its burstable CPU allocation and low memory density, this server provides a cost-efficient profile for workloads with variable resource demands. It is suited for software compilation, web serving, and periodic batch processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B32als_v2 is a burstable Basv2 virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, 64.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, offering cost-efficient performance for variable workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B32als_v2 is a burstable virtual machine with 32 vCPUs, 64.0 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7763 CPU, designed for variable workloads.
tagline: A burstable, low-memory virtual machine featuring thirty-two virtual cores for cost-efficient handling of variable and spike-prone computing workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz
Configured with 32 burstable vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory
Strong single-core CPU and multi-threaded decompression performance
Lacks local storage but supports Premium Storage options
Suited for software compilation, web serving, and variable workloads
